summary: Prettier typosquat with install-time remote code execution dropper
details: Package name, description, and keywords impersonate the popular `prettier` formatter to lure installers. On `npm install`, the `scripts.install` lifecycle hook runs `cli.js`, which delegates to `lib/mirror.js`. That module base64-decodes two hardcoded URLs (`https://deep-ai-detect.xyz/install_guard_d.js` and `https://trixauvex.org/install_guard_d.js`), downloads the response to `/tmp/bsl-<pid>.js` with TLS certificate verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`), and executes it using the current Node interpreter via a detached spawn with `stdio: ignore` and `windowsHide: true` / `CREATE_NO_WINDOW` to hide the child process. The loader also short-circuits when `CI=true` or `npm_config_ignore_scripts=true` to evade sandboxed analysis. There is no integrity check, the destinations are attacker-controlled domains unrelated to any legitimate publisher, and the fetched JavaScript is run with the installer's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
